title: Roghni Naan

seo.title: Roghni Naan Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Flatbread
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Roghni Naan with our easy-to-follow Pakistani recipe. Learn how to make this soft, flavorful, sesame-studded flatbread at home. Perfect for any meal!
seo.h1: Authentic Roghni Naan Recipe: Soft, Fluffy Pakistani Flatbread
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A plate of freshly baked Roghni Naan, garnished with sesame seeds and cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy warm, fluffy Roghni Naan, a traditional Pakistani flatbread, perfect with curries or kebabs.

description: Roghni Naan is a rich, soft, and aromatic leavened flatbread from Pakistan, often adorned with sesame seeds. Its distinct texture and subtle sweetness make it a perfect accompaniment to savory curries and kebabs. This traditional bread is a staple in Pakistani households, bringing warmth and flavor to any meal.

instructions.title: Activate Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm milk, sugar, and yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy, indicating the yeast is active. This is the first step for a fluffy naan.

instructions.title: Prepare Dough
instructions.text: Add the all-purpose flour, salt, beaten egg, yogurt, and 2 tablespoons of melted ghee or butter to the yeast mixture. Mix everything together until a shaggy dough forms. Gradually add 1/4 to 1/2 cup water, a tablespoon at a time, if the dough is too dry, until it comes together.

instructions.title: Knead Dough & First Proof
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Form it into a ball, place it in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5-2 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape Naans
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough and divide it into 4-6 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. On a lightly floured surface, flatten each ball into an oval or teardrop shape, about 1/4 inch thick.

instructions.title: Second Proof & Toppings
instructions.text: Place the shaped naans on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Cover them loosely and let them rest for another 15-20 minutes. Just before baking, lightly brush each naan with water or milk, then generously sprinkle with nigella seeds and sesame seeds.

instructions.title: Bake Naans
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to its highest setting (around 450-500°F or 230-260°C) with a baking stone or inverted baking sheet inside for at least 30 minutes. Carefully transfer the naans to the hot surface and bake for 3-5 minutes per side, or until golden brown and puffed up, with slightly charred spots. Bake in batches as needed.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the naans from the oven and immediately brush them with a little extra melted ghee or butter for shine and flavor. Serve your Roghni Naan warm with your favorite Pakistani curries, gravies, or kebabs.

tips: For a truly authentic texture, use a tandoor if available. Otherwise, a very hot oven with a preheated baking stone or inverted baking sheet will mimic the high heat needed for perfectly puffed naans.
While ghee adds a rich flavor, unsalted butter can be used as a substitute. For extra richness and flavor, use full-fat yogurt.
Roghni Naan is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, sprinkle with a little water, wrap in foil, and reheat in a warm oven or on a griddle for a few minutes to restore softness.
article: Roghni Naan is a beloved Pakistani Baking dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
